title: Veggie Power Takes Over October 1
story: Every October 1, people around the world celebrate World Vegetarian Day by ditching meat and trying delicious plant-based foods instead! Restaurants create special veggie menus, families cook colorful meals with beans and vegetables, and social media fills with photos of amazing meat-free dishes. Some people use this day to try being vegetarian for the first time, while others share their favorite recipes with friends.
activity_question: What do restaurants often do on World Vegetarian Day?
follow_up_question: What vegetarian dish would you most want to try making?

title: Celebrating Our Wise and Wonderful Elders
story: Every October 1, people around the world celebrate International Day of Older Persons to honor grandparents, great-grandparents, and all the amazing older adults in our communities. Families visit nursing homes, share stories with elderly neighbors, and post photos with their favorite older relatives on social media. Schools organize special programs where kids interview seniors about life decades ago, learning incredible tales from before smartphones and video games existed!
activity_question: What do kids often do with seniors during this celebration?
activity_choices: Interview them about life in the past
Challenge them to video game contests
Ask them to do their homework
activity_answer: Interview them about life in the past
follow_up_question: What amazing story might an older person in your family tell you?

title: Spin Into CD Player Day!
story: Every October 1, music lovers celebrate CD Player Day by dusting off their old compact disc players and spinning their favorite albums! People share photos of their vintage CD collections on social media, hunt for rare discs at thrift stores, and remember when skipping songs meant actually pressing a button. Some families have CD listening parties where everyone brings their most treasured disc to play. It's a nostalgic day that proves sometimes the old ways of enjoying music still rock!
activity_question: What do people often hunt for at thrift stores on CD Player Day?
bonus_fact: CDs can hold about 74 minutes of music - enough for most full albums!
